short_description.value: Copernicus-FM is a unified Earth observation foundation model capable of processing any spectral or non-spectral sensor modality from all major Copernicus Sentinel missions, using dynamic hypernetworks and flexible metadata encoding. It is pretrained on the Copernicus-Pretrain dataset (18.7M images) and evaluated on the Copernicus-Bench benchmark (15 hierarchical downstream tasks).

pretext_training_type.value: Masked image modeling (MIM) with auxiliary continual distillation

pretraining.value: Pretrained on Copernicus-Pretrain (220K grids with all modalities, 100 epochs) using masked image modeling and continual distillation from DINOv2 (RGB) and SoftCon (S1/S2). Dynamic hypernetworks generate patch embedding weights for spectral and non-spectral modalities. Metadata (location, area, time) is integrated via Fourier encoding.

value.value: Language model encoding for variable names
Dynamic patch embedding via spectral and variable hypernetworks
Global land and near-land ocean, including polar regions

token_size.value: Varies by modality: S1/2 (16x16), S3 (8x8), S5P (4x4), DEM (64x64)

sampling.value: Gaussian sampling around top-10K populated cities (std=50km), uniform for polar regions, fill gaps for missing grids

processing_level.value: S1: GRD, S2: TOA, S3: OLCI, S5P: L2, DEM: GLO-30

cloud_cover.value: S3: filter tiles with >20% bright pixels; S5P: filter fully NaN tiles; S1/2: not specified

missing_data.value: Remove corrupted/NaN-dominated images; S5P NaNs replaced with zero
